title: A Japanese photographer’s striking, perceptive images of the Troubles | Aeon Videos
text:
EXCLUSIVEA Japanese photographer’s striking, perceptive images of the TroublesBorn into an elite Japanese family, the photographer Akihiko Okamura (1929-85) was an unlikely candidate to become one of the most perceptive chroniclers of the Troubles in Northern Ireland. A collaboration between Photo Museum Ireland and the Akihiko Okamura Archive, The Memories of Others traces how the photographer came to create original and thoughtful depictions of the decades-long sectarian conflict, many of which remained largely unseen until recently. Combining archival footage, interviews and Okamura’s photographs, the short documentary reveals how a deeply rooted sense of justice led him to embrace empathy and eschew spectacle, resulting in images that juxtapose everyday life with extraordinary violence.Directors: Pauline Vermare, Marc Lesser
Producers: Noah Avidan, Marc Lesser, Pauline Vermare
